name: Hearty Black Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 48 ounces canned black beans (frijoles negros), drained and rinsed
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin, or to taste
16 ounces canned whole tomatoes, drained and chopped
1/4 cup fresh tomatoes, peeled and chopped
salt and freshly ground pepper – to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan over medium heat, warm the oil.
2. Add onions and cook until soft, about 3 to 5 minutes.
3. Stir in garlic and cook for an additional minute.
4. Add black beans, oregano, cumin, thyme, bay leaf, ham, stock, sherry, and whole tomatoes.
5. Increase heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, about 30 minutes.
7. Season with salt and pepper as needed.
8. Serve soup with desired accompaniments or cool to room temperature and refrigerate or freeze.
Makes about 7 cups.
Timesaver Tip: Soup can be prepared up to 2 days ahead, covered, and refrigerated, or frozen. To freeze, pour soup into one large or several smaller freezer containers, cover, label, and freeze at 0°F for up to 1 month. To serve, thaw soup in the microwave on Defrost setting for 15-20 minutes or in the refrigerator for 8-24 hours. Gently reheat soup in a saucepan or microwave at 50% power until warmed through, stirring occasionally during defrosting and reheating. Microwave Version: Combine oil with onions in a 2-quart casserole on 70% power for 45 seconds, then add garlic and cook for an additional 3 minutes until onions are soft. Add remaining ingredients as directed, cover with waxed paper or plastic wrap, and microwave on 70% power for 30 minutes. Season before serving according to the steps above.
